themes.hook: India just gave an Iranian warship a parking spot. It wasn't a friendly visit—it was a multi-billion dollar insurance policy for our energy supply.
key_data_points: 80% of India's oil imports transit through the Strait of Hormuz
8-10% potential hit to daily City Gas Distribution (CGD) volumes from Middle East disruptions
Iran considering cargo passage specifically for payments settled in Yuan
themes.non_obvious_angle: Most analysts focus on the price of crude. The real story is the 'logistics-for-sovereignty' trade-off. India is using naval diplomacy as a physical hedge against the Strait of Hormuz blockade because financial hedges (like oil futures) don't solve physical supply shortages.
themes.your_take_prompt: What's your stance on India potentially settling energy trades in Yuan to bypass the Hormuz blockade, and does this undermine the 'Rupee Internationalization' narrative?

source_articles.title: India has given docking to Iranian Warship
Middle East disruptions to impact daily CGD sales volume by 8-10%
Iran considering limited tanker passage if paid in yuan

themes.hook: Maharashtra is literally selling its teak forests to pay for monthly welfare checks. This is what happens when fiscal consolidation meets electoral desperation.
key_data_points: ₹1,500 monthly payout to low-income women under the Majhi Ladki Bahin Yojana
Central government creating an 'Economic Stabilisation Fund' for fiscal headroom
Commitment to the Fiscal Consolidation Roadmap despite global headwinds
themes.non_obvious_angle: There is a widening 'Fiscal Schizophrenia' between the Center and States. While the Center builds stabilization funds to look disciplined for global rating agencies, States are liquidating long-term natural capital (forests) to fund recurring revenue expenditures. We are trading 50-year-old trees for 30-day consumption cycles.
themes.your_take_prompt: Is the 'Stabilisation Fund' just a PR exercise for rating agencies if state-level deficits are being plugged by selling off the environment?

source_articles.title: Maharashtra considering raising funds from teak forests for welfare scheme
FM Says India Won't Go Back On Fiscal Consolidation Roadmap
Proposed Economic Stabilisation Fund to provide fiscal headroom

themes.hook: For a decade, 'Make in India' solar meant gluing together Chinese parts. That era of 'screwdriver manufacturing' just hit its expiration date in Nagpur.
key_data_points: 10 GW Ingot and Wafer facility being set up by Waaree Energies
India currently imports almost all of its solar wafers, primarily from China
themes.non_obvious_angle: The real moat in renewables isn't the assembly of panels; it's the 'ingot-to-wafer' chemistry. By moving upstream, India isn't just chasing 'Atmanirbhar' slogans—it's insulating its entire energy transition from the next inevitable US-China trade war.
themes.your_take_prompt: Does Waaree’s massive scale suggest that the PLI schemes are finally working for deep-tech manufacturing, or is this just a desperate move to beat future tariffs?

source_articles.title: Waaree Energies to Set Up India’s Largest 10 GW Ingot & Wafer Facility
Waaree Energies Announces India's Largest 10 GW Integrated Facility

themes.hook: What if the 'India Growth Story' of the last decade is mathematically wrong? New data suggests we overestimated growth by nearly 2% since 2012.
key_data_points: 2005-2011 growth potentially underestimated by 1-1.5 percentage points
2012-2023 growth potentially overestimated by 1.5-2 percentage points
Fitch still projects FY26 GDP growth at 7.5% driven by domestic demand
themes.non_obvious_angle: Data credibility is the ultimate macro risk. If our 'boom' years were actually stronger and our 'miracle' years are actually weaker, then our entire capital allocation strategy—from infra spending to corporate Capex—is built on a legacy of miscalculation. We might be optimizing for a reality that doesn't exist.
themes.your_take_prompt: If the PIIE data is right, how much of our current 'bull market' is based on inflated growth expectations?

source_articles.title: New evidence suggests India misestimated annual economic growth rate
FITCH Says See India FY26 GDP Growth At 7.5%

themes.hook: Trump’s 'Section 301' isn't just a headline for news anchors. It’s a legal trigger that can shut down a textile factory in Punjab or a diamond hub in Surat overnight.
key_data_points: Section 301 of the Trade Act targets 16 economies including India
6-7% revenue growth expected for diamantaires if tariff relief is granted
US court tariff rulings have delayed the signing of a major India-US trade deal
themes.non_obvious_angle: We talk about 'Trade Wars' in the abstract, but they function as surgical strikes on specific Indian MSME clusters. The 'complexity' in trade talks isn't about global policy; it's about the survival of low-margin industrial hubs that rely on 2-3% tariff differentials to remain globally competitive.
themes.your_take_prompt: Why is the Indian government holding off on the US trade deal—is it a position of strength or a failure to negotiate around Section 301?

source_articles.title: Why is Trump targeting 16 economies? Section 301 explained
For diamantaires, tariff relief spells 6-7% revenue growth
India will hold off on signing a trade deal with the United States
